correct_answer: Coral reefs are often referred to as the 'rainforests of the sea' due to their incredible biodiversity.
retrieved_context: CORAL REEFS Reducing greenhouse gas emissions is crucial to mitigate the impacts of climate change on coral reefs GREENHOUSE GAS EMISSIONS. CORAL REEFS Coral reefs play a vital role in supporting fisheries by providing essential habitats for a wide variety of fish species and other marine organisms. These habitats are crucial for the sustainability of local and global fishing industries, contributing significantly to food security. By offering shelter, breeding grounds, and feeding areas, coral reefs ensure the health and abundance of fish populations that are vital for the fishing industry, thereby underlining the interconnectedness of coral reef ecosystems and fisheries. FISHERIES. CORAL REEFS Marine protected areas are established to safeguard coral reefs from human activities and environmental stressors MARINE PROTECTED AREAS. CORAL REEFS Coral reefs are made up of coral polyps that secrete calcium carbonate to form their intricate structures CORAL POLYPS. CORAL REEFS Coral reefs support an estimated 25% of all marine species, playing a key role in marine biodiversity MARINE BIODIVERSITY. CORAL REEFS Coral reefs are a fundamental part of marine ecosystems, providing shelter and food for a wide variety of marine life MARINE ECOSYSTEMS. CORAL REEFS Coral reefs provide crucial coastal protection services that benefit coastal communities COASTAL COMMUNITIES. CORAL REEFS Agricultural runoff is smothering corals and introducing harmful chemicals, impacting coral reefs AGRICULTURAL RUNOFF. CORAL REEFS Plastic waste introduces harmful chemicals into the marine environment, impacting coral reefs PLASTIC WASTE. CORAL REEFS Implementing sustainable fishing practices is necessary to protect coral reefs and ensure the health of fisheries SUSTAINABLE FISHING PRACTICES. CORAL REEFS Ocean acidification, exacerbated by climate change, is damaging coral reefs OCEAN ACIDIFICATION. CORAL REEFS Destructive fishing practices damage coral reefs and deplete fish populations DESTRUCTIVE FISHING PRACTICES. CORAL REEFS Overfishing depletes fish populations that rely on coral reefs, threatening their ecosystem OVERFISHING. CORAL REEFS Climate change is causing coral bleaching and death, threatening coral reefs CLIMATE CHANGE. CORAL REEFS Coral reefs attract tourists for diving and snorkeling, generating significant revenue for the tourism industry and supporting local economies TOURISM INDUSTRY. CORAL REEFS Pollution from land-based sources significantly impacts coral reefs, contributing to their decline. It is essential to reduce pollution in order to improve water quality in coastal areas and protect coral reefs. Addressing the issue of pollution is crucial for the health and sustainability of these vital marine ecosystems. POLLUTION.
